data.narrative:
Evozyne is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $144M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same stage, supplemented by 3 public sector comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples is not applicable due to lack of disclosed revenue for the private target.

Two triangulation methods yield actionable results. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $144M total raised, implying a $274M valuation. The Peer-Median method anchors to five same-stage biotech peers—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yielding a median valuation of $810M and a range of $600M–$3.3B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous technology maturity and capital efficiency within Series B biotech.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $233M–$931M, with a median of $542M. The Stage-Progression method produces a more conservative floor, while Peer-Median reflects sector-wide valuations. Key uncertainties include Evozyne's stage-specific progress metrics, competitive differentiation versus the peer cohort, and capital efficiency relative to peers. Any valuation above $810M would require demonstrable competitive advantages or accelerated milestone achievement.
